items.title: L7’s Jennifer Finch Reveals Brain Cancer Diagnosis
items.summary: A few months ago, the great '90s grunge-punk band L7 announced plans for a farewell tour. Today, singer and bassist Jennifer Finch has shared the news that she won't be able to take part in the tour because she's been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer. The 59-year-old Finch has already been through multiple surgeries.
items.audio: audio/L7_s Jennifer Finch Reveals Brain Cancer Diagnosis.mp3

items.title: The Shins Celebrate 30th Anniversary With Previously Unreleased Songs On New 7″
items.summary: The Shins' debut album Oh, Inverted World turned 25 this summer, but the band had been going for five years by the time that dropped. The wistfully chirping indie rockers are celebrating three decades with a new limited edition 30th anniversary 7" featuring previously unreleased songs. The post The Shins Celebrate 30th Anniversary With Previously Unreleased Songs On New 7″ appeared first on Stereogum.
items.audio: audio/The Shins Celebrate 30th Anniversary With Previous.mp3

items.title: Credit Card Announce Debut Mixtape Devil’s Night / Dead Internet: Hear “Harsh Times”
items.summary: SEO-unfriendly Detroit synth-punks Credit Card have a reportedly wild live show that makes heavy use of a strobe light and a subwoofer. Their sound is a quasi-industrial low-tech grind that combines abrasive vocals with throbbing beats. Last month, the band dropped their Germs-referencing debut 7" (CC).
items.audio: audio/Credit Card Announce Debut Mixtape Devil_s Night .mp3

items.title: L7’s Jennifer Finch diagnosed with brain cancer; GoFundMe launched
items.summary: “Following multiple surgeries and serious complications, Jennifer now requires extensive medical care, rehabilitation and professional in-home support.” – L7

items.title: Swervedriver talk ‘Raise at 35’ Tour, shoegaze revival, more (BV Interviews)
items.summary: “The great thing is it’s just riffs. It’s wall to wall riffs,” Swervedriver’s Adam Franklin says of the band’s 1991 debut album, Raise. Swervedriver’s Adam Franklin and Jimmy Hartridge are on the July 13 episode of the BV Interviews podcast.

items.title: Mastodon announce new LP ‘Marrow Deep’ (exclusive vinyl & new single ft. QOTSA’s Josh Homme)
items.summary: It’s their first album since the death of Brent Hinds last year which they also discussed further in a short documentary they released last week.
